jokes.title: Which is the most used tank on the battlefield?
jokes.body: The fuel tank

jokes.title: I have a few more tank jokes...
jokes.body: ...but I might go off track

jokes.title: Knock knock!
jokes.body: Who's there? Tank. Tank who? You're welcome!

jokes.title: Why don't tanks ever get lost?
jokes.body: They always follow their tracks.

jokes.title: What's a tank's favorite music?
jokes.body: Heavy metal, of course.

jokes.title: Why are tanks always calm under pressure?
jokes.body: They have thick armor and zero depression.

jokes.title: What do tanks eat for breakfast?
jokes.body: Panzer cakes!

jokes.title: What do you call a tank that tells jokes?
jokes.body: A pun-zer.

jokes.title: Why don't tanks ever forget anything?
jokes.body: They have excellent track records.

jokes.title: Why don't tanks play hide and seek?
jokes.body: They always stand out in the field.

jokes.title: Why did the tank get promoted?
jokes.body: Because it has high caliber skills.

jokes.title: What's a tank's favorite exercise?
jokes.body: Running on tracks.

jokes.title: Two goldfish are in a tank.
jokes.body: One looks to the other and says, 'Do you know how to drive this thing?'

jokes.title: How do tanks show gratitude?
jokes.body: They say: Tank you very much!

jokes.title: Code Name 'Tank'
jokes.body: The name "tank" was introduced in 1915 as a security measure to conceal their true purpose. British workers were told they were building mobile water tanks for the Mesopotamian front.

jokes.title: Leonardo da Vinci's Design
jokes.body: In the 15th century, Leonardo da Vinci designed a cone-shaped fighting vehicle powered by men turning cranks, often considered a proto-tank.

jokes.title: Little Willie
jokes.body: The first completed prototype tank, "Little Willie", was built in 1915. It weighed 16 tons and could only move at about 3.2 km/h (2 mph).

jokes.title: First Battle Usage
jokes.body: Tanks were first used in combat by the British Army on September 15, 1916, at the Battle of the Somme.

jokes.title: The Renault FT
jokes.body: The French Renault FT (1918) was the first operational tank to feature a fully rotating turret, setting the standard layout for modern tanks.

jokes.title: British Mark I Tank
jokes.body: The British Mark I, introduced in 1916, was the world’s first production combat tank, built to cross trenches and barbed wire on the Western Front in World War I.

jokes.title: Cambrai 1917 Massed Tank Attack
jokes.body: On 20 November 1917 at the Battle of Cambrai, Britain used over 476 Mark IV tanks in the first large-scale, coordinated tank assault, proving that concentrated armor could break trench defenses.

jokes.title: Blitzkrieg and Panzer Divisions
jokes.body: In 1939-1940, German Panzer divisions using tanks like the Panzer III and IV, combined with motorized infantry and air support, executed fast "Blitzkrieg" offensives in Poland and France.

jokes.title: Soviet T-34 Medium Tank
jokes.body: The Soviet T-34, entering service around 1940-1941, used sloped armor, a powerful gun, and wide tracks, becoming one of the most influential and mass-produced tanks of World War II.

jokes.title: German Tiger I Heavy Tank
jokes.body: Introduced in 1942, the German Tiger I had thick armor and an 88 mm gun, making it fearsome in combat, but it was expensive, mechanically unreliable, and difficult to deploy in large numbers.

jokes.title: Battle of Kursk
jokes.body: The Battle of Kursk in July-August 1943 is often regarded as the largest tank battle in history, involving thousands of German and Soviet tanks such as Panthers, Tigers, and T-34s on the Eastern Front.

jokes.title: Rise of the Main Battle Tank
jokes.body: After World War II, armies gradually replaced separate light, medium, and heavy tank classes with the "main battle tank" concept, seen in designs like the British Centurion, U.S. Patton series, and Soviet T-54/55.
